title: Standardized proof envelope with application/bolyra+json content type

artifacts.description: Canonical specification for application/bolyra+json: field definitions, required vs optional fields, versioning rules, content-type registration rationale, and interop requirements for LangChain/CrewAI/AutoGen.

10+ JSON test vectors covering all three circuits (AgentPolicy, Delegation, HumanUniqueness), both proving systems (groth16, plonk), valid and invalid envelopes, and edge cases (missing fields, wrong version, malformed publicSignals).
TypeScript implementation of ProofEnvelope type, validateProofEnvelope() with Zod schema, serializeEnvelope(), deserializeEnvelope(), and CONTENT_TYPE constant ('application/bolyra+json').
JSON Schema (draft-07) for the proof envelope, consumable by non-TS integrators and used for spec conformance runner.
Python dataclass + pydantic model for ProofEnvelope with validate_proof_envelope(), serialize_envelope(), deserialize_envelope(), and CONTENT_TYPE constant.
Mocha/Chai tests: round-trip serialization for all circuit types, validateProofEnvelope() acceptance and rejection against all spec/test-vectors, content-type header assertion.
Pytest tests mirroring the TS suite: round-trip, validation against shared test vectors, pydantic model coercion checks.
Extend existing conformance runner to load spec/test-vectors/proof-envelope/ and assert that both TS and Python SDKs accept/reject each vector per its expected outcome field.

Developer-facing guide: envelope anatomy, how to wrap an existing proof, how LangChain/CrewAI/AutoGen integrations should attach content-type headers, and upgrade path from pre-envelope ad-hoc formats.

steps: Draft spec/proof-envelope-v1.md: define envelope schema with required fields (version, circuit, proof, publicSignals) and optional metadata (provingSystem, timestamp, chainId, nonce), enumerate allowed circuit and provingSystem enum values, specify content-type string 'application/bolyra+json', and document versioning policy.
Write sdk/src/envelope.schema.json as the normative JSON Schema artifact derived from the spec; this becomes the single source of truth for validation in both SDKs.

Implement sdk/src/envelope.ts: import zod, define ProofEnvelope TypeScript type from JSON Schema, implement validateProofEnvelope() that throws typed errors on violations, add serializeEnvelope() / deserializeEnvelope() helpers, export CONTENT_TYPE constant.

Implement sdk-python/bolyra/envelope.py: define ProofEnvelope pydantic BaseModel with field validators matching the JSON Schema, implement validate_proof_envelope(), serialize_envelope(), deserialize_envelope(), export CONTENT_TYPE.

Author 10+ test vectors in spec/test-vectors/proof-envelope/: at minimum one valid vector per circuit+provingSystem combination (AgentPolicy/groth16, AgentPolicy/plonk, Delegation/groth16, Delegation/plonk, HumanUniqueness/groth16), plus invalid vectors for missing 'version', unknown 'circuit', malformed 'proof' object, non-array 'publicSignals', and future version string; each vector carries an 'expected' field ('valid' or error code).

Write sdk/test/envelope.test.ts: load all test vectors from spec/test-vectors/proof-envelope/, run validateProofEnvelope() on each and assert accept/reject matches expected; add round-trip tests for serializeEnvelope/deserializeEnvelope; assert CONTENT_TYPE equals 'application/bolyra+json'.
Write sdk-python/tests/test_envelope.py: same vector-driven pattern using pytest parametrize; share vector loading logic via a conftest fixture pointing at spec/test-vectors/.
Extend spec/conformance-runner.ts to discover and execute proof-envelope vector tests against both SDK implementations, reporting per-vector pass/fail for CI gating.
Update integrations/langchain/ middleware to wrap all tool outputs in a ProofEnvelope and set Content-Type header; update integrations/crewai/ and any AutoGen adapter similarly.

Update sdk/src/index.ts and sdk-python/bolyra/__init__.py to re-export validateProofEnvelope, serializeEnvelope, deserializeEnvelope, CONTENT_TYPE so integrators access them from the top-level package.
Write docs/proof-envelope.md covering envelope anatomy, usage examples for TS and Python, framework integration snippets, and migration notes for existing ad-hoc formats.
Run full test suite (npm test + pytest) and conformance runner; verify all vectors pass; commit with DCO sign-off.
dependencies: spec/test-vectors/ directory convention (establish if not present)
spec/conformance-runner.ts (extend existing or create skeleton first)
